title: A Family Race to Remember a Sister
summary: Margo and Mark did not like each other when they first met over 40 years ago. Margo thought Mark was too serious, and Mark thought Margo was too loud. But they both loved Julia, who was Margo's sister and Mark's wife. Julia got sick and passed away in 2022. Before she died, she wanted Margo and Mark to do something special together. So they joined a TV show called Race Across the World. In this race, teams travel without phones or planes and with little money to win a prize. Margo and Mark became the first in-laws to compete. They raced to honor Julia's last wish and to become closer as family. The race was hard, but it helped them remember Julia and work together.

people who are related, like sisters and husbands

questions.question: What did Julia want before she died?

background_read: This story is about family and loss. Margo and Mark are in-laws, which means they are connected through marriage to Julia. Julia was Margo's older sister and Mark's wife. She had a rare blood cancer and died in 2022. Before her death, she wished for Margo and Mark to do something together to bond. They chose to enter Race Across the World, a challenging TV competition where teams race across countries without using phones, internet, or airplanes, and with a small budget. The show tests teamwork and endurance, and the first team to finish wins £20,000. This race helped Margo and Mark honor Julia and improve their relationship.
Article_Structure: WHO: Margo Oakley and Mark Blythen, in-laws who did not get along at first.
WHAT: They competed in Race Across the World, a TV race without modern tools, to honor Julia's last wish.
WHERE: The race happens in various locations, but they are from the UK.
WHY: To remember Julia, who died, and to become closer as family.

perspectives.description: The race helped Margo and Mark overcome their differences and strengthen their family ties after a loss.
